title: Bartlingia sessilis
intro: Bartlingia sessilis, also known as the small-fruited Bartlingia, is a plant species belonging to the family Santalaceae. The health benefits of Bartlingia sessilis include its potential to lower blood sugar levels and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Bartlingia sessilis is used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, and is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments, including diabetes and digestive issues.
